ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employment_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region of the data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data entry */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the data collection (e.g., Monthly) */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate as a percentage */,
  "employed" REAL /* Number of employed individuals */,
  "labour_participation_rate" REAL /* Labour participation rate as a percentage */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of area (e.g., Rural, Urban) */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the population (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the population (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the population */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the population (e.g., High School, Bachelor's, Master's) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_frequency FOREIGN KEY ("frequency") REFERENCES frequencies ("frequency_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Andhra Pradesh) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "gdp" REAL /* Gross Domestic Product of the region */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the region record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the region record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "frequencies" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frequency type */,
  "frequency_name" TEXT /* Name of the frequency (e.g., Monthly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the frequency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unemployment_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unemployment rate record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the unemployment rate entry */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate as a percentage */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region for the unemployment rate */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the unemployment rate record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the unemployment rate record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_unemployment_rates_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistics record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the statistics */,
  "average_unemployment_rate" REAL /* Average unemployment rate for the year */,
  "total_employed" INTEGER /* Total number of employed individuals for the year */,
  "average_labour_participation_rate" REAL /* Average labor participation rate for the year */,
  "total_unemployed" INTEGER /* Total number of unemployed individuals for the year */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the statistics record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the statistics record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_statistics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
